headline: Solar Canals Cut Water Loss by 70 Percent
meta_description: Solar panels above California irrigation canals reduced water evaporation by 70% while generating clean electricity and reducing algae growth.
summary_short: Solar panels installed over California canals cut water evaporation by 70% while producing clean energy for the region.
body:
California's Central Valley faces constant pressure to balance water needs with scarce supplies. A state-funded pilot project found an elegant two-fold solution: installing solar panels above irrigation canals. The results have been striking. Over a full irrigation season, the panels reduced water evaporation by up to 70 percent while simultaneously cutting aquatic weed and algae growth by up to 85 percent.

Beyond water conservation, the panels generate clean electricity—a genuine win-win that turns a routine piece of agricultural infrastructure into a resource-saving innovation. This pilot demonstrates how thoughtful design can solve multiple problems at once, protecting precious water supplies while advancing renewable energy in one of the nation's most productive agricultural regions.
